scope_note: QA Five Families Complete Partition cert [398]. Primary sources: Wall (1960) 'Fibonacci series modulo m' American Mathematical Monthly 67(6):525-532 DOI: doi.org/10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano period pi(m), period-24 for m=9, period-8 for m=3); Lucas (1878) 'Theorie des fonctions numeriques simplement periodiques' Amer. J. Math. 1:184-240 (Lucas sequences, generalized Fibonacci families); Iverson (1993) 'Pythagorean Arithmetic' Vols I-III (BEDA framework: b,e,d=b+e,a=b+2e; five-family seed classification; digital-root orbit structure); Dale (2026) 'A Complete Classification of Pythagorean Triples via Generalized Fibonacci Sequences and Pisano Periods' (Five Families paper; Theorem 2 = Complete Partition; Table 1 = 9x9 classification; path: papers/ready-for-submission/pythagorean-five-families/pythagorean_five_families_paper.tex). CLAIM (= Theorem 2 of the Five Families paper, narrow and falsifiable): The five generalized Fibonacci sequences with seeds (1,1), (2,1), (3,1), (3,3), (9,9), evolved under digital-root recurrence dr(b,e)->(e,dr(b+e)), produce consecutive digital-root pair orbits of sizes 24, 24, 24, 8, 1 that are pairwise disjoint, cover all 81 = 9^2 digital-root pairs {1,...,9}^2 exactly once, and whose 9x9 classification table matches Table 1 of the Five Families paper exactly. Checks: (C1) each orbit has the stated period (24/24/24/8/1) — exact integer arithmetic; (C2) no early closure (period is minimal); (C3) pairwise disjoint — all 10 intersection checks pass; (C4) complete coverage — union equals {1,...,9}^2; (C5) table match — all 81 cells match the paper's Table 1. QA structure: the five families partition the QA state space {1,...,9}^2 (A1 No-Zero) under digital-root T-step = mod-9 shadow of QA T-operator. Cosmos (72 = 24+24+24 pairs): Fibonacci+Lucas+Phibonacci. Satellite (8 pairs): Tribonacci. Singularity (1 pair): Ninbonacci. Theorem NT: T-step is T(b,e)=(e,dr(b+e)) with dr(n)=((n-1)%9)+1 — pure integer. No floats. No continuous functions. Classification labels are string tags (observer side). Extends cert [281] (Pisano-Orbit Correspondence, pi(3)=8 and pi(9)=24); cert [212] (Fibonacci Hypergraph, orbit multiset (24,24,24,8,1)); cert [211] (Cayley Bateson Filtration, component sizes). This cert is the COMPUTATIONAL PROOF of the paper's Theorem 2 — the paper's proof sketch is computational; this cert implements it with full verification. 5 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-12.
